title: Lab 45: Restore Default SELinux Context
description: Restore the default SELinux context for a web file on servervm and keep httpd enforcing.

tasks: On servervm, the file /var/www/html/index45.html has the wrong SELinux context. Restore the default context.
Ensure the httpd service is enabled and running on servervm. SELinux must remain enforcing.
hints: Use the default file-context policy instead of a custom override.
This lab now runs on servervm.
checks: ssh admin@servervm getenforce | grep -qx Enforcing && ssh admin@servervm systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled && ssh admin@servervm systemctl is-active httpd | grep -qx active
solution_outline: Restore the default context for index45.html.
Enable and start httpd while keeping SELinux enforcing.
task_titles: Restore the default context on servervm
Enable httpd on servervm with SELinux enforcing
